root_text.sn45.13:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn45.13:1.4: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, sekkho hotī”ti?
root_text.sn45.13:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, sekkhāya sammādiṭṭhiyā samannāgato hoti …pe… sekkhena sammāsamādhinā samannāgato hoti.

translation_text.sn45.13:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn45.13:1.3: “Sir, they speak of the one called ‘a trainee’.
translation_text.sn45.13:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s someone who has a trainee’s right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.13:2.2: That’s how a trainee is defined.”
